steps.title: Find what makes the calendars different
steps.content:
The student's goal is to emphasize a DIFFERENCE between the two calendars.

Among the notes, one statement directly identifies a difference: "the timelines for the months don't match".

This is reinforced by examples such as Ventóse (FR month 6) aligning with February (Gregorian month 2), and Floréal (FR month 8) aligning with April (Gregorian month 4).

steps.title: Pick the choice that highlights that difference
steps.content:
C directly identifies and exemplifies the difference in month timelines — the correct fit for the goal.

  • A — "Used in France from 1793 to 1805, the French Republican calendar includes the months of Ventóse and Floréal (the sixth and eighth months of the year)." Describes only the French Republican calendar; no comparison to the Gregorian is made, so no difference is emphasized.
  • B — "The second and fourth months of the Gregorian calendar are February and April, respectively." Describes only the Gregorian calendar; no comparison is made.
  • D — "The French Republican calendar, which replaced the Gregorian calendar in France from 1793 to 1805, has twelve months, including the months of Ventóse and Floréal." Mentions both calendars but emphasizes a shared feature (twelve months) and replacement history rather than a difference in how their month timelines align.

That matches Choice C — The timelines for the months of the two calendars don't match; for example, the sixth month of the French Republican calendar begins during the second month of the Gregorian calendar..
